text: Under MARPOL Annex VI, SECA stands for Sulfur Oxide ($SO_x$) Emission Control Area. Which regions are designated SECAs?

options.text: The North Sea area and the English Channel
Both A and B are correct

explanation: Sulfur Oxide Emission Control Areas (SECAs) have been designated in specific regions to limit $SO_x$ emissions from ships. The Baltic Sea, the North Sea, and the English Channel are recognized SECAs where ships must use low-sulfur fuel or exhaust gas cleaning systems.
